content.text: Let me verify both changes look correct in context:

Both edits are exactly right. Here's a summary of what was added to `common/chat.cpp`:

---

### What was added

**Handler function** (inserted at line 1634, just before `common_chat_try_specialized_template`):

```cpp
static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
        const common_chat_template & tmpl,
        const autoparser::generation_params & inputs)
```

- Calls `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl` to render the prompt through the Jinja template as-is (the template itself handles system-prepend-to-first-user-turn and `</s>` after assistant turns)
- Sets `format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE`, `supports_thinking = false`
- Registers `[INST]`, `[/INST]`, and `</s>` as preserved tokens so the tokenizer won't split them
- Builds a minimal PEG parser: anchors on the rendered `generation_prompt` then captures the rest as content — appropriate because this format has no tool-call or thinking tokens to intercept mid-generation

**Detection block** (appended inside `common_chat_try_specialized_template`, just before `return std::nullopt`):

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos && src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v0.3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

- Placed **after** every existing handler so it doesn't disrupt any of them
- The `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` absence check is the explicit distinguisher from the Ministral 3 handler (which requires `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` to be present)
- No existing handler was touched; the header file was not touched

result:
Both edits are exactly right. Here's a summary of what was added to `common/chat.cpp`:

---

### What was added

**Handler function** (inserted at line 1634, just before `common_chat_try_specialized_template`):

```cpp
static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
        const common_chat_template & tmpl,
        const autoparser::generation_params & inputs)
```

- Calls `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl` to render the prompt through the Jinja template as-is (the template itself handles system-prepend-to-first-user-turn and `</s>` after assistant turns)
- Sets `format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE`, `supports_thinking = false`
- Registers `[INST]`, `[/INST]`, and `</s>` as preserved tokens so the tokenizer won't split them
- Builds a minimal PEG parser: anchors on the rendered `generation_prompt` then captures the rest as content — appropriate because this format has no tool-call or thinking tokens to intercept mid-generation

**Detection block** (appended inside `common_chat_try_specialized_template`, just before `return std::nullopt`):

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos && src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v0.3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

- Placed **after** every existing handler so it doesn't disrupt any of them
- The `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` absence check is the explicit distinguisher from the Ministral 3 handler (which requires `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` to be present)
- No existing handler was touched; the header file was not touched
